content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Robert Poythress 
3125 Forest Ct. 
Madera, CA 9363 7 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-215 
Dear Mr. Poythress: 
December 15, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your duties as a County Supervisor 
for Madera County under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act")1 
and Section 1090. 
As required by Section l 097 .1 ( c )( 5), we have forwarded a copy of your request to both the 
Attorney General's Office and the Madera County District Attorney's Office. Section 1097 .1 ( c )( 4) 
provides that: "When issuing the advice or opinion, the Commission shall ... provide to the person 
who made the request a copy of any written communications submitted by the Attorney General or 
a local district attorney .... " Therefore, we enclose a response from the Madera County District 
Attorney's office regarding Section 1090 and limited our advice to the Act. If you have questions 
concerning the District Attorneys advice you should contact him. We have not received a 
communication from Attorney General's Office. 
QUESTION 
As a member of the Board of Supervisors, would you have a conflict of interest in approving 
the budget of the Treasurer ff ax Collector where the Treasurerff ax Collector maintains County 
funds in an account at the bank at which you are employed? 
CONCLUSION 
While, under Section 87100, you would have a conflict of interest in decisions that 
foreseeably and materially affect your employer, the decisions on the Treasurer/fax Collector 
budget would not have a foreseeable financial effect on your financial interests. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-16-215 
Page No. 2 
You are the elected Madera County Supervisor for District 3 in Madera and will assume 
office during the first week of January, 2017. In your private capacity, you are employed by 
Citizens Business Bank as Manager of the Madera, California office. You intend to continue your 
employment at the bank. 
Currently, the County has an interest earning account at your bank. This account was 
established several years ago and is controlled by the Treasurerffax Collector Office. The 
Treasurer/Tax Collector position is an independent elected position. 
Deposits into the Treasurerffax Collector's account contribute to the profitability of your 
office. You also have the potential ofreceiving a bonus each year based on your office's annual 
earnings. On October 17, 2016, you provided the following additional information. 
• You also own stock in the bank. The bank is publically traded and there are over I 06 million 
shares outstanding as of December 31, 2015. Your ownership consists of about 14,000 
shares (or .013 percent of the outstanding shares), some acquired through the bank's profit 
sharing program, some acquired independently. 
• The County funds on deposit with the bank are not under a competitive bid contract. The 
deposits were placed through you three to four years ago. 
On October 26, 2016, you stated: 
• The Bank has approximately 800 employees. 
• You have worked at the bank for almost 12 years. You position at the bank is an officer 
(vice president) but you are not a bank director. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one 
or more of the public official's interests. Section 87103 also sets forth the interests from which a 
conflict of interest may arise under the Act. Section 87103 includes the following: 
• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 
or more. (Section 87103(b).) 


File No. A-16-215 
Page No. 3 
• Any source ofincome, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103( c ). ) Note that 
clients of the bank may also be a source of income where they have contributed to 
"incentive compensation" you receive. "Incentive compensation" means income received by 
an official who is an employee, over and above salary, which is either ongoing or 
cumulative, or both, as sales or purchases of goods or services accumulate. (Regulation 
18700.l(d).) Incentive compensation is calculated by a predetermined formula set by the 
official's employer that correlates to the conduct of the purchaser in direct response to the 
effort of the official.2 
• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $460 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months 
prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).) 
• Personal finances, meaning the financial effect of a governmental decision on the personal 
finances of a public official or his or her immediate family. 
Of these interests, the only interests implicated by your facts are your interest in your employer 
under Sections 87103(a), (c) and (d). 
Foreseeability a11d Materiality 
A conflict of interest only exists if the decision in question will foreseeably and materially 
affect your interests, including your employer or other sources of income. In analyzing whether the 
decision in question will foreseeably and materially affect your interests, Commission regulations 
provide two standards of foreseeability depending on the facts and circumstances surrounding the 
governmental decision. 
• 
Explicit ln\'0/vement: First, a financial efect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the 
financial interest is a named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision before the 
official or the official's agency. (Regulation 18701 (a).) A financial interest is the subject of 
2 "Incentive compensation" does not include: salary; commission income; bonuses for activity not related to 
sales or marketing, the amount of which is based solely on merit or hours worked over and above a predetennined 
minimum; and such executive incentive plans as may be based on company perfonnance, provided that the formula for 
determining the amount of the executive's incentive income does not include a correlation between that amount and 
increased profits derived from increased business with specific and identifiable clients or customers of the company. 
Incentive compensation also does not include payments for personal services that are not marketing or sales. The 
purchaser is a source of income to the official if all three of the following apply: (1) the official's employment 
responsibilities include directing sales or marketing activity toward the purchaser; (2) there is direct personal contact 
between the official and the purchaser intended by the official to generate sales or business; and (3) there is a direct 
relationship between the purchasing activity of the purchaser and the amount of the incentive compensation received by 
the official. (Regulation 18700.l(d).) 


File No. A-16-215 
Page No. 4 
a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of 
any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest, and 
includes specified effects on property interests listed in Regulation I 8702.2(a)(I )-(6). Your 
interests are explicitly involved in the decision in question. 
• In all other cases, Regulation 18701(b) provides the standard applicable when an official's 
financial interest is not explicitly involved in a decision, such as yours. In general, if the 
financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and is more than hypothetical or 
theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable. A financial effect need not be likely to be 
considered reasonably foreseeable, but if the financial result cannot be expected absent 
extraordinary circumstances not subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably 
foreseeable. 
Under your facts, the Bank would be explicitly involved in decisions related to the 
agreement to maintain the account at the bank. Moreover, they would be a named party in cases 
where the the deposit and/or the bank is otherwise set forth in city documents under the 
consideration of the Board of Supervisors. Materiality is presumed under such circumstances. 
However, in all other decisions, where the bank or the account are not named or otherwise 
the subject of a proceeding (such as funding the Treasurerrrax Collector's Office), it is only 
foreseeable if the decision could realistically and materially affect the the account or the bank. 
Regulation 18702.1 (b) sets forth the materiality standard used to make this determination -- the 
financial effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change in the price 
of the business entity's publicly traded stock, or the value of a privately-held business entity in the 
value of the business entity. 
Note that when a public official holds an office specified in Section 87200 (such as a 
member of the Board of Supervisors) has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public 
meeting, he or she must: ( l) immediately prior to the discussion of the item, orally identify each 
type of economic interest involved in the decision as well as details of the economic interest on the 
record of the meeting; (2) recuse himself or herself, and (3) leave the room for the duration of the 
discussion and/or vote on the item. (Section 87105.) 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
,, 
I By: 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
John W. Wallace 
Assistant General Counsel, 
\ 
Legal Division
